sn17.1:1.1: Thus have I heard. On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's Park. There the Blessed One addressed the bhikkhus thus: "Bhikkhus!" sn.ii.226
sn17.1:1.2: "Venerable sir!" those bhikkhus replied. The Blessed One said this:
sn17.1:2.1: "Bhikkhus, dreadful are gain, honour, and praise, bitter, vile, obstructive to achieving the unsurpassed security from bondage.[n.316] Spk: Gain (lābha) is the gain of the four requisites; honour (sakkara), the gain of (requisites) that are well made and well produced; praise (siloka), acclamation (vaṇṇaghosa). Therefore, bhikkhus, you should train yourselves thus: ‘We will abandon the arisen gain, honour, and praise, and we will not let the arisen gain, honour, and praise persist obsessing our minds.’ Thus should you train yourselves."
